title: Peri Didaxeon: source lineage for CME00117
summary: CME00117 is the checked-in repository representation of the University of Michigan encoding of Max Löweneck's 1896 Peri Didaxeon. U-M selected Löweneck's title page and English text on printed odd pp. 3-53, omitting his German introduction, facing Latin comparison text, final annotations, and advertisements. Löweneck states that his English edition is based directly on British Library Harley MS 6258 B, ff. 51v-66v; Cockayne's earlier edition supplied explicitly marked comparison readings, not a hidden transcription layer. Modern scholarship identifies the Old English work as an adaptive version of the older Latin L/S/H branch called Tereoperica, rather than simply a translation of De Renzi's R-branch Practica attributed to Petrocello. The sole manuscript copy is late twelfth century and imperfect at the end. The full currently published Internet Archive PDF/OCR/metadata set for its scanned Löweneck exemplar is cached, but it represents the edition only partially because the exemplar lacks printed introduction pp. i-v; exact tested BookReader boundaries are n8 for the encoded title and n14-n64 for the encoded English text. Complete public provider PDFs and metadata are also cached and bounded for Cockayne volume III (Peri Didaxeon at printed pp. 81-145 / PDF pp. 123-187) and De Renzi volume IV (Practica at printed pp. 185-286 / PDF pp. 193-294). No currently usable legitimate complete public manuscript facsimile was found: the current British Library catalog says images are unavailable, legacy viewer links redirect to a generic page, and plausible IIIF and aggregator searches did not yield an auditable target object.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00117
entities.description: The checked-in XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID are all CME00117; the file is 110,976 bytes.
The XML has a TEMPHEAD change entry dated 9-12-16 with username lattaj and responsibility MURP.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00117
entities.description: Public electronic text representing Löweneck's work title page and the English/right-hand text on printed odd pp. 3-53. It omits the surviving German introduction, facing Latin comparisons, annotations pp. 54-57, and advertisements.

entities.label: Peri Didaxeon (Old English medical work)
entities.description: The anonymous post-Conquest Old English prose medical translation and compilation: a short historical and theoretical epistle followed by clinical and therapeutic remedies organized a capite ad calcem. The sole surviving copy is incomplete at the end. This abstract work layer is distinct from its manuscript, editions, digital encodings, and images.

date_statements.label: Translation and compilation
date_statements.value: Probably late eleventh or early twelfth century
date_statements.notes: The exact date is disputed; this is a work date, not the 1160-1199 date of the surviving witness.

notes: Extant only in an imperfect copy; no complete ending is known.

entities.label: Tereoperica Latin medical textual tradition
alternate_labels: L/S/H branch of the first book later attributed to Petrocello
entities.description: The older Latin medical text branch transmitted by Paris, Bibliothèque nationale de France, lat. 11219 (L), British Library Sloane 2839 (S), and British Library Harley 4977 (H). Maion names it Tereoperica from the title in the oldest witness and shows that Peri Didaxeon aligns closely with this branch. It is distinct from De Renzi's R/V/P branch edition and from a proven direct exemplar for the Old English translator.

date_statements.label: Oldest cited witness
date_statements.value: Mid-ninth century in Paris, BnF lat. 11219
date_statements.notes: This attestation predates the twelfth-century physician Petrocello and makes the traditional attribution doubtful.

entities.label: Löweneck's 1896 Peri Didaxeon
entities.description: The immediate scholarly edition represented by U-M and CME00117. Löweneck transcribed Harley MS 6258 B directly, moved manuscript marginal notes and glosses into footnotes, marked Cockayne's divergent readings C., and printed Latin comparison passages opposite the English text.

bibliographic.citation: Max Löweneck, ed., Peri Didaxeon: Eine Sammlung von Rezepten in englischer Sprache aus dem 11./12. Jahrhundert. Nach einer Handschrift des Britischen Museums, Erlanger Beiträge zur englischen Philologie 12 (Erlangen: Fr. Junge, 1896).
bibliographic.title: Peri Didaxeon: Eine Sammlung von Rezepten in englischer Sprache aus dem 11./12. Jahrhundert

physical_description.extent: viii + 57 printed pages, plus series matter and advertisements in the scanned bound volume
notes: The edition prints Latin comparison passages on facing even pages and the English text on odd pages.
The available scanned exemplar lacks printed introduction pp. i-v even though the edition's bibliographic extent is viii + 57.

entities.label: Cockayne's 1866 first printed Peri Didaxeon text
entities.description: Cockayne's earlier manuscript-based edition in Leechdoms, Wortcunning, and Starcraft. Löweneck did not silently copy it as his base text: he says his own edition rests directly on Harley MS 6258 B and records Cockayne's divergent readings in footnotes with C.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Oswald Cockayne, ed., Leechdoms, Wortcunning, and Starcraft of Early England, vol. 3, Rolls Series 35 (London, 1866), Peri Didaxeon at pp. 81-145.
bibliographic.title: Leechdoms, Wortcunning, and Starcraft of Early England
bibliographic.pages: Peri Didaxeon at pp. 81-145; Old English text begins p. 82

notes: The complete three-volume series spans 1864-1866; Peri Didaxeon is specifically in volume III (1866), with its sectional title on p. 81, Old English text beginning on p. 82, and the final Old English and English text on pp. 144-145.
Internet Archive's item metadata supplies the series start date 1864, but direct inspection of the cached volume-III title page establishes 1866 for this edition entity.

Cockayne's opening header reads 'MS. Harl. 6258. fol. 83 b.=42 b.' at printed p. 82 (PDF p. 124; BookReader n123), and his text later marks fol. 98 b. at printed p. 142 before ending on p. 144. The current British Library catalog instead locates the same target at ff. 51v-66v. These are retained as target-specific historical/current endpoint layers; no whole-codex foliation conversion is inferred.

entities.label: De Renzi's 1856 Practica Petrocelli edition
entities.description: The Latin comparison edition used primarily for Löweneck's facing text. De Renzi edited the R witness, Paris BnF lat. 14025, in Collectio Salernitana IV. Maion places R with V and P in a branch distinct from the older L/S/H Tereoperica branch closest to the Old English work; De Renzi is therefore a comparative printed intermediary, not the Old English translator's direct source.

bibliographic.citation: Salvatore De Renzi, ed., Collectio Salernitana, ossia Documenti inediti, e trattati di medicina appartenenti alla scuola medica salernitana, vol. 4 (Naples: Tipografia del Filiatre-Sebezio, 1856), 185-286.
bibliographic.title: Collectio Salernitana

notes: Internet Archive's item metadata supplies the series start date 1852, but direct inspection of the cached volume-IV title page establishes 1856 for this edition entity.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 6258 B
entities.description: Late-twelfth-century English parchment medical miscellany. Peri Didaxeon is its third main work, at ff. 51v-66v and more exactly f. 51v/23-f. 66v/23. The text breaks off and a lower-margin catchword plus gathering number shows that at least one further quire once followed.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.notes: Witness production date, not work composition date.

holding.notes: The work-level component record is 041-002162301; manuscripts are consulted at the British Library in London subject to registration and advance request.

physical_description.extent: v + 66 + v leaves; modern pencil foliation ff. 1-66
notes: Produced in England and written as one codex in Insular minuscule with Caroline and Protothic features; EM1060 identifies one scribe for the target work.
The manuscript is imperfect at the beginning and end, has lost leaves and fire-damaged leaves, and was rebound in 1972.
Peri Didaxeon occupies sixteen folios in the current catalog, ff. 51v-66v.

Cockayne's 1866 edition labels the target opening 'fol. 83 b.=42 b.' and later marks fol. 98 b.; those historical foliation labels crosswalk to the current catalog's target endpoints but are not normalized into a general offset beyond Peri Didaxeon.

entities.label: British Library legacy digital viewer for Harley MS 6258 B
entities.description: A historically linked British Library manuscript viewer, cited by EM1060 and formerly addressable by folio. In 2026 the exact viewer URLs redirect to a generic digitised-manuscripts page, the current catalog says images are unavailable, and the sequence's completeness, canvas count, image endpoints, and license cannot be audited.
entities.reproduction_format: Legacy web manuscript image sequence; current technical boundaries unavailable

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Löweneck's 1896 Peri Didaxeon
entities.description: Wellcome Library-contributed public scan and Internet Archive derivatives. The provider's PDF has 72 pages derived from a 74-image, 300-ppi scan and is complete as the currently published provider file. Its scanned exemplar includes bound series matter, the work title, introduction pp. vi-viii, parallel edition pp. 2-57, and advertisements, but lacks printed introduction pp. i-v.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page images with derived plain-text OCR and JSON item/page-number metadata

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Cockayne's Leechdoms, volume III
entities.description: Harold B. Lee Library-contributed public scan of the complete 1866 volume III and Internet Archive derivatives. The cached 516-page provider PDF includes the Peri Didaxeon sectional title and bilingual edition at printed pp. 81-145, mapped to PDF pp. 123-187.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 516-page PDF page-image surrogate with JSON item metadata
notes: Brigham Young University sponsored the scan; the provider metadata's date 1864 is a series-level value and does not override the volume-III title page date 1866.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of De Renzi's Collectio Salernitana, volume IV
entities.description: Royal College of Physicians, London-contributed public scan of the complete 1856 volume IV and Internet Archive derivatives. The cached 638-page provider PDF includes the complete Practica Petrocelli at printed pp. 185-286, mapped to PDF pp. 193-294.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 638-page PDF page-image surrogate with JSON item metadata
notes: Jisc and Wellcome Library sponsored the scan; the provider metadata's date 1852 is a series-level value and does not override the volume-IV title page date 1856.

entities.label: Maion's 2002 Peri Didaxeon source and translation study
entities.description: Danielle Maion's detailed study of the sole manuscript, date, structure, language, practical-medical organization, translation practices, and Latin textual tradition. It corrects Löweneck's treatment of the final catchword and replaces simple dependence on De Renzi's Petrocello attribution with the older L/S/H Tereoperica branch.

bibliographic.citation: Danielle Maion, 'Il trattato medico antico inglese Peri Didaxeon: problemi di traduzione,' Traduzione, società e cultura 10 (Trieste, 2002), 1-30.
bibliographic.title: Il trattato medico antico inglese Peri Didaxeon: problemi di traduzione
bibliographic.publisher: Università degli Studi di Trieste, Dipartimento di scienze del linguaggio

agents.name: British Library
alternate_names: British Museum (historical holding name in Löweneck's title)

agents.name: Harold B. Lee Library
notes: Named as the contributing library for Internet Archive item leechdomswortcun03cock; Brigham Young University is named as sponsor.

access.access_method: Obtain a British Library Reader Pass and submit the Manuscripts request form for consultation in London, citing Harley MS 6258 B and target ff. 51v-66v.
Use the British Library image-order form with shelfmark Harley MS 6258 B and exact target ff. 51v-66v; specify complete present-object or target-folio color photography and intended reproduction use.
access.format: Reference or reproduction-quality digital images; existing surrogate, microfilm scan, or new photography as available
notes: The British Library may supply an existing surrogate, including black-and-white microfilm-derived scans unless color is requested; original-manuscript scanning may be unavailable and photography may be substituted.
Preserve the delivered files, invoice, order scope, and item-specific reuse terms before any publication or Git use.

rights.component: Late-eleventh-/early-twelfth-century Old English and embedded Latin textual work
rights.basis: The work is directly attested in a late-twelfth-century manuscript, and the current U-M item is marked Public Domain / No Copyright-United States. This statement concerns the medieval wording, not manuscript photographs, modern catalog prose, modern scholarship, digital interfaces, or repository markup.

rights.component: Medieval Latin textual work and textual tradition
rights.basis: Maion identifies a mid-ninth-century witness to the Latin branch. This conclusion concerns the medieval Latin wording, not modern editions, transcriptions, catalogs, or images.

rights.component: Löweneck's 1896 edition text, introduction, and apparatus
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1896, and both the current U-M item and Internet Archive item describe the represented publication as public-domain/no-copyright material. This does not determine rights in later scans, provider metadata, or interfaces.

rights.component: Cockayne's 1866 volume-III edition text, translation, notes, and apparatus
rights.basis: The cached title page establishes publication of volume III in 1866. This conclusion concerns Cockayne's historical edition layer, not later scans, provider metadata, or interfaces.

rights.component: De Renzi's 1856 volume-IV edition text, commentary, and apparatus
rights.basis: The cached title page establishes publication of volume IV in 1856. This conclusion concerns De Renzi's historical edition layer, not later scans, provider metadata, or interfaces.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan derivatives and item-level provider assertion
rights.basis: The exact Internet Archive metadata applies the Public Domain Mark 1.0 to item b30591855. A separate item note says 'title is copyright,' so the mark is recorded as a provider assertion, not a warranty for every derivative or embedded component.
notes: A Public Domain Mark is a status mark, not a license or legal warranty.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan derivatives and item metadata for leechdomswortcun03cock
rights.basis: The exact Internet Archive item metadata has no rights or license field. Public download availability and the public-domain status of the 1866 edition do not by themselves establish the scan layer's legal status.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan derivatives and item-level provider assertion for b24758346_0004
rights.basis: The exact Internet Archive item metadata applies the Public Domain Mark 1.0. The mark is recorded as the provider's item-level status assertion, not as a license or legal warranty for every derivative or embedded component.
notes: A Public Domain Mark is a status mark, not a license or legal warranty.

rights.basis: The item and collection rights layer state Public Domain / No Copyright-United States and provide educational and research access. Provider terms continue to govern the interface and service.

rights.component: Kyvero-Press/CME repository packaging and modern XML markup for CME00117 only
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository root LICENSE dedicates the repository's 'software' to the public domain under the Unlicense, and no narrower notice was found under source/CME_phase_3. The license is retained as evidence, but its wording does not resolve whether CME00117's data and modern XML markup are included in that software scope. This unknown status is limited to the repository-artifact layer and does not alter the separately modeled medieval text, editions, U-M encoding, scans, catalog, manuscript images, or Maion article.
notes: The separate source/CME_from_OTA/read_me_license.txt applies to that named path and was not extended to source/CME_phase_3.
Do not broaden the root software dedication to XML data or markup without an XML-specific or path-specific statement from the repository owner.

rights.component: Physical medieval manuscript as an object
rights.basis: Copyright status of the medieval wording does not itself govern custody, reader access, handling, photography, catalog data, or reproductions of the physical object.

rights.component: British Library legacy and any newly supplied manuscript images
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and any proposed place of reuse
rights.basis: The target image sequence is currently unavailable, so no item-specific image rights label or delivery license could be inspected. British Library website and imaging terms apply, and reproduction permission or delivery-specific conditions may be required.
notes: Do not infer image rights from the public-domain medieval text or from the catalog-text license.

rights.component: British Library Archives and Manuscripts catalog descriptive text
rights.basis: The current catalog states that its text is © British Library Board and CC BY except where otherwise stated. This catalog-data layer is distinct from the manuscript and its images.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution, as stated by the catalog

rights.component: Maion's 2002 article and the complete Traduzione, società e cultura 10 provider PDF
rights.basis: The volume copyright page states © 2002 Dipartimento di scienze del linguaggio, della traduzione e dell'interpretazione. Public download does not by itself grant an open reuse license.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Harley MS 6258 B text, manuscript corrections, marginal remarks and glosses, plus Cockayne's prior readings
editorial_practices.representation: Direct base transcription; manuscript marginalia moved to footnotes; Cockayne variants marked C.
editorial_practices.description: Löweneck explicitly states that he used the manuscript onsite and based his edition directly on it. He moved marginal observations and glosses into footnotes and recorded Cockayne's divergent readings with C.
editorial_practices.scope: English text printed on odd pp. 3-53 and its footnotes

editorial_practices.source_feature: Latin passages compared with the Old English text
editorial_practices.representation: Facing even-page excerpts primarily from De Renzi's Practica Petrocelli edition, with Gariopontus and Isidore discussed for particular passages
editorial_practices.description: The parallel Latin pages are an editorial source comparison. Later research shows De Renzi's R witness belongs to a different branch from the older L/S/H Tereoperica branch closest to Peri Didaxeon.
editorial_practices.scope: Facing Latin/even printed pages and introduction source discussion

editorial_practices.representation: Work title page and English/right-hand text only
editorial_practices.description: The electronic text encodes the work title page and English odd-page text through p. 53. It omits the surviving German introduction, facing Latin comparison pages, final annotations pp. 54-57, and advertisements.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Löweneck's final numbered heading based on the lower-margin words and Roman numeral VII at Harley f. 66v
editorial_practices.representation: DIV2 TYPE='item' N='67' with the catchword in HEAD and an empty P
editorial_practices.description: Löweneck and the encoding treat the f. 66v material as textual item 67. Maion identifies it instead as a catchword and gathering number for a lost next quire; descriptive structure therefore separates it from the 66 surviving prose items.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00117.xml.
evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD, IDG, FRONT, BODY, two DIV1 layers, 67 DIV2 items, and complete descendants
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for repository identity, U-M handle, exact 1896 title and imprint, editor credit, selected edition layers, headings, notes, page milestones, intrinsic Latin, and final catchword representation.

evidence.citation: Independent structural and boundary audit of CME00117.xml against Löweneck's cached scan.
evidence.locator: Element counts, sequential DIV2 numbering, PB REF crosswalk, title/body boundaries, first five and final five headings, and empty item-67 P
evidence.summary: Confirms 110,976 bytes; two DIV1; 67 sequential DIV2 TYPE='item' numbered 1-67; 78 HEAD, 150 MILESTONE, 309 NOTE, 70 P, 27 PB, 1,672 HI, two SUP, and no GAP, UNCLEAR, LG, or L. The 27 PB elements cover the edition title and English printed odd pages rather than every parent-edition page.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: git blob fdddb29aa445bf3d6a5d843d6dd77e10a9f99657; root Unlicense dedication
evidence.summary: The root license dedicates the repository's software to the public domain under the Unlicense. No narrower notice is present under source/CME_phase_3, while the separate CC BY-NC-SA notice under source/CME_from_OTA is path-specific and is not extended to CME_phase_3. Because the root wording does not state whether XML data or markup is software, it supports recording the license but not a resolved public-domain status for the CME00117 repository artifact.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Peri Didaxeon, CME00117.
evidence.summary: Stable item identity, Löweneck responsibility, 1896 Erlangen source, content navigation, public text access, and Public Domain / No Copyright-United States rights layer.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection description.
evidence.summary: Collection-level description of approximately 300 texts, public-domain-edition sources, 2016-2018 manual keying, in-house proofreading/editing, XML encoding, bulk access, and provider terms. It is not treated as item-specific proof where the CME00117 file supplies more exact evidence.

evidence.citation: Max Löweneck, ed., Peri Didaxeon, Erlanger Beiträge zur englischen Philologie 12 (Erlangen: Fr. Junge, 1896), Internet Archive b30591855.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 9-69; title, introduction pp. vi-viii, parallel text pp. 2-53, annotations pp. 54-57
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for bibliography, title, direct onsite manuscript use, marginalia-to-footnote policy, Cockayne variants, Latin comparison sources, printed English text, and the final catchword treated as text.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive derived OCR for b30591855.
evidence.locator: Introduction sections 3-6, parallel text, footnotes, and annotations
evidence.summary: Searchable supporting evidence for Löweneck's source argument and direct-manuscript/editorial-method statement; visual claims were checked against the PDF rather than accepted from OCR alone.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for b30591855.
evidence.locator: metadata, rights, notes, scanner, sponsor, contributor, and files arrays
evidence.summary: Identifies the Wellcome Library sponsor/contributor, 2019 scan, 74 images at 300 ppi, PDF/OCR derivatives, ARK, Public Domain Mark field, bibliographic extent, and conflicting 'title is copyright' note.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive page-number map for b30591855.
evidence.locator: BookReader n8 (work title), n14 (printed p. 3, English text start), and n64 (printed p. 53, English text end)
evidence.summary: Supplies the provider's leaf-to-print-page mapping for the encoded span. Direct provider images at all three boundary leaves were independently inspected and the tested BookReader links returned the expected pages.

evidence.citation: Direct page-by-page boundary inspection of cached Internet Archive item b30591855 against CME00117 PB structure.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 1-72; title p. 9 / BookReader n8; introduction pp. 10-12; half-title p. 13; printed pp. 2-57 at PDF pp. 14-69; encoded English pp. 3-53 at PDF pp. 15-65 / BookReader n14-n64; ads pp. 70-72
evidence.summary: Confirms a complete 72-page provider PDF and exact work-to-file mapping. The exemplar begins the introduction at printed p. vi and lacks i-v. CME00117 selects the title and English odd pp. 3-53 and omits the German introduction, facing Latin, annotations, and ads. Tested BookReader boundary links agree with the cached page-number map and direct page images.

evidence.citation: Thomas Oswald Cockayne, ed., Leechdoms, Wortcunning, and Starcraft of Early England, vol. III (London, 1866), Internet Archive leechdomswortcun03cock.
evidence.locator: volume-III title page at PDF p. 9; Peri Didaxeon at printed pp. 81-145 / PDF pp. 123-187 / BookReader n122-n186; historical manuscript labels fol. 83b (=42b) at p. 82 and fol. 98b at p. 142
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for the volume-III date 1866 and the complete first printed Peri Didaxeon section. The section title is on printed p. 81; the Old English starts on p. 82 under 'MS. Harl. 6258. fol. 83 b.=42 b.'; fol. 98b is marked on p. 142; and the final Old English and English text appears on pp. 144-145 before the next work begins.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for leechdomswortcun03cock.
evidence.locator: metadata, files, contributor, sponsor, scanner, volume, publicdate, and rights fields
evidence.summary: Identifies volume 3, ARK ark:/13960/t3b00778m, Harold B. Lee Library as contributor, Brigham Young University as sponsor, the 2012 provider publication, and the complete provider file inventory. The item has no rights or license field and gives the series-level date 1864, which is not substituted for the inspected volume-III title-page date 1866.

evidence.citation: Direct page-boundary inspection of cached Internet Archive item leechdomswortcun03cock.
evidence.locator: complete PDF pp. 1-516; volume-III title p. 9; Peri title PDF p. 123 / BookReader n122; opening header PDF p. 124 / n123; fol. 98b marker PDF p. 184 / n183; Old English end PDF p. 186 / n185; translation end PDF p. 187 / n186
evidence.summary: Confirms that the cached provider PDF is the complete 516-page volume, that the title page dates it 1866, and that Peri Didaxeon occupies printed pp. 81-145 / PDF pp. 123-187. Direct inspection distinguishes the sectional title at n122 from the manuscript transcription at n123, whose header reads 'MS. Harl. 6258. fol. 83 b.=42 b.'; Cockayne marks fol. 98b on printed p. 142 and the Old English target ends on p. 144. The current British Library catalog's ff. 51v-66v is retained as a separate current foliation layer, and no conversion is extrapolated beyond the target.

evidence.citation: Salvatore De Renzi, ed., Collectio Salernitana, vol. IV (Naples, 1856), Internet Archive b24758346_0004.
evidence.locator: volume-IV title page at PDF p. 5; Practica Petrocelli at printed pp. 185-286 / PDF pp. 193-294 / BookReader n192-n293
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for the volume-IV date 1856 and the complete Practica Petrocelli source segment used comparatively by Löweneck. The following Fragmenta secundi libri begins at printed p. 287 / PDF p. 295.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for b24758346_0004.
evidence.locator: metadata, files, contributor, sponsor, scanner, volume, publicdate, and rights fields
evidence.summary: Identifies volume 4, ARK ark:/13960/t9w12nc64, Royal College of Physicians, London as contributor, Jisc and Wellcome Library as sponsors, the 2015 provider publication, the complete provider file inventory, and the item-level Public Domain Mark. Its series-level date 1852 is not substituted for the inspected volume-IV title-page date 1856.

evidence.citation: Direct page-boundary inspection of cached Internet Archive item b24758346_0004.
evidence.locator: complete PDF pp. 1-638; volume-IV title p. 5; Practica PDF pp. 193-294; tested BookReader leaves n192 and n293
evidence.summary: Confirms that the cached provider PDF is the complete 638-page volume, that the title page dates it 1856, and that Practica Petrocelli occupies printed pp. 185-286 / PDF pp. 193-294. Direct provider images at BookReader n192 and n293 show the Practica opening and ending pages respectively.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Peri Didaxeon, Harley MS 6258 B, ff. 51v-66v, record 041-002162301.
evidence.locator: Full component description, access, digital-content, and rights notices
evidence.summary: Exact shelfmark and folios, unique Old English translation, Latin source-family description, 1160-1199 date, English/Old English and Latin languages, opening and breaking-off loci, imperfect ending, current manuscript-request route, and notice that digital images are currently unavailable.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 6258 B, Medical miscellany, record 040-002052106.
evidence.summary: Parent codex identity and sequence; England and late-twelfth-century production; Peri Didaxeon ff. 51v-66v; parchment, extent, dimensions, foliation, collation, script, losses, fire damage, 1972 binding, and provenance.

evidence.citation: The Production and Use of English Manuscripts 1060 to 1220, London, British Library, Harley 6258B.
evidence.locator: Peri Didaxeon locus and manuscript description
evidence.summary: Exact f. 51v/23-f. 66v/23 locus, one-scribe assessment, corrections and revisions, late-twelfth-century dating, v + 66 + v extent and collation, and historical links to a British Library viewer and Doane microfiche facsimile.

evidence.citation: Danielle Maion, 'Il trattato medico antico inglese Peri Didaxeon: problemi di traduzione,' Traduzione, società e cultura 10 (Trieste, 2002), 1-30.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 53-82; article pp. 1-30; source discussion pp. 11-15
evidence.summary: Codex-unicus status, witness and probable work dates, manuscript errors, lost-ending catchword, work structure and contents, a capite ad calcem order, clinical/therapeutic scheme, bilingual register, transitional southwestern language, Latin witness branches, doubtful Petrocello attribution, and close but adaptive relationship to Tereoperica.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation services and current manuscript request routes.
evidence.summary: Current registered-reader physical-consultation route and fee-based reference/reproduction imaging route. The service may use existing surrogates, including black-and-white microfilm-derived scans, or photography where the original cannot be scanned.

evidence.citation: British Library website and Imaging Services terms.
evidence.summary: Current ordering, delivery, payment, access, and reproduction conditions; image rights and permitted uses depend on item and delivery terms, while the public catalog separately licenses its descriptive text under CC BY except where stated.

evidence.citation: Dated complete-public-facsimile search for British Library Harley MS 6258 B, performed 2026-07-13.
evidence.locator: Current child and parent catalogs; exact legacy Viewer/FullDisplay references; plausible BL IIIF identifiers; Biblissima, Internet Archive, exact-shelfmark, facsimile, digital-surrogate, and microfilm searches; EM1060 historical links
evidence.summary: The current component catalog explicitly says digital images are unavailable. Exact f. 1r and f. 51v legacy viewer URLs now redirect to a generic collection page; plausible BL IIIF manifests return 404; no current legitimate full object was found through Biblissima, Internet Archive, or exact-shelfmark searches. EM1060 documents a historical viewer and microfiche facsimile, so the negative result is current-access evidence, not proof that no institutional surrogate exists.
notes: No image was downloaded and no provider control was bypassed.
Because no complete public object is currently usable, no folio-to-canvas or image-URL boundary can be truthfully recorded.

open_questions.question: Can the former British Library viewer object or another complete, foliation-stable facsimile of Harley MS 6258 B be restored, supplied, or newly photographed?
next_steps: Ask the British Library whether its legacy viewer or existing microfilm/digital surrogate can be restored or supplied for Harley MS 6258 B.
If ordering images, request the complete present object plus covers and flyleaves and identify ff. 51v-66v separately; request color explicitly if black-and-white microfilm would be insufficient.
After lawful delivery, checksum every file, preserve the invoice and license, and map exact folio, canvas/file, start, and end boundaries before claiming completeness.
notes: No inquiry was sent during this review. Negative search evidence is dated and does not establish destruction or institutional absence.

open_questions.question: Which lost Latin exemplar or intermediary state underlies Peri Didaxeon, and can the work's translation date and place be narrowed beyond probable late-eleventh-/early-twelfth-century England?
next_steps: Consult Maion's 1999 critical edition and subsequent work on the complete Latin tradition.
Compare Peri Didaxeon with Paris BnF lat. 11219, British Library Sloane 2839, and British Library Harley 4977 without assuming any extant witness was the direct exemplar.
Keep Löweneck's De Renzi/Gariopontus/Isidore comparisons, the Tereoperica source family, manuscript date, and southwestern scribal features as distinct evidence layers.

open_questions.question: Can a leaf-complete Löweneck 1896 exemplar be found, and what exactly does Internet Archive's item-level Public Domain Mark cover given the metadata note 'title is copyright'?
next_steps: Locate a second institutional copy or scan containing introduction pp. i-v and compare the entire volume with b30591855.
Continue to label each cached derivative complete for its provider source file while describing the scanned edition portion as missing pp. i-v.
Seek provider clarification before treating either the Public Domain Mark or the conflicting note as a legal warranty for all derivatives.

open_questions.question: What item-specific rights and delivery conditions would govern British Library images of Harley MS 6258 B for repository storage, public web display, and print-on-demand use?
next_steps: Request written item-specific terms with any image order and preserve them alongside the files.
Ask separately about research reference use, public web redistribution, and print-on-demand reproduction.
Do not transfer the medieval text's public-domain status or the catalog text's CC BY statement to manuscript images.

review_notes: Source-file identity was established first from the checked-in XML, then traced through the U-M item to the represented edition and exact manuscript locus.
A complete public-manuscript-facsimile search was performed without bypassing provider controls; no target image object was cached because none was currently available.
Root and path-specific CME license notices were checked before assigning the modern XML-markup rights layer.
